variations.text: The United States adopts a federal ban on all single-use plastic products including packaging and containers, with strict enforcement penalties and only narrow medical exemptions.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: The United States adopts a sweeping federal ban on all single-use plastic products, packaging, and synthetic disposable materials, with zero exemptions, mandatory compliance audits, and severe criminal penalties for violations.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases in both the scope of materials covered and the strictness of enforcement. Tier 1 is merely voluntary guidelines on two items; Tier 2 introduces a binding ban but only on select items with broad exemptions; Tier 3 matches the original with a general ban on single-use plastics; Tier 4 explicitly extends to packaging and containers with strict penalties and narrow exemptions; Tier 5 eliminates all exemptions, broadens to synthetic disposable materials, and adds severe criminal penalties and mandatory audits.

original_text: Georgia introduces mandatory financial literacy courses for high school graduation.

variations.label: minimal
variations.text: Georgia introduces a mandatory bare-bones financial literacy overview module for high school graduation, covering only the most basic definitions of saving and budgeting.

variations.label: low
variations.text: Georgia introduces a mandatory introductory financial literacy course for high school graduation, offering a surface-level survey of common personal finance topics.

variations.label: medium
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory financial literacy courses for high school graduation.

variations.label: high
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory rigorous financial literacy courses for high school graduation, featuring in-depth coverage of budgeting, investing, taxes, credit, and retirement planning with applied projects.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory expert-level comprehensive financial literacy courses for high school graduation, encompassing advanced investing strategies, tax optimization, estate planning, macroeconomic analysis, and real-world simulations developed with leading financial professionals.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the depth, rigor, and comprehensiveness of the financial literacy course requirement. Tier 1 is a minimal overview module with the most superficial content; tier 2 is a surface-level introductory course; tier 3 matches the original neutral statement; tier 4 adds rigorous in-depth coverage with applied components; tier 5 reaches expert-level instruction with advanced topics and professional-grade simulations. The core scenario (Georgia mandating financial literacy for graduation) is preserved throughout.

original_text: A U.S. federal law eliminates the student loan interest rate for all borrowers.

variations.label: minimal

variations.label: low
variations.text: A U.S. federal law cuts the student loan interest rate in half for all borrowers.

variations.label: medium
variations.text: A U.S. federal law eliminates the student loan interest rate for all borrowers.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A U.S. federal law eliminates the student loan interest rate for all borrowers and retroactively cancels all previously accrued interest.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: A U.S. federal law eliminates the student loan interest rate for all borrowers, retroactively refunds all interest ever paid, and applies a negative interest rate that reduces outstanding principal over time.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier represents a monotonically increasing degree of interest rate reduction: tier 1 is a modest reduction, tier 2 halves it, tier 3 (the original) eliminates it entirely, tier 4 extends the elimination retroactively to accrued interest, and tier 5 goes further by refunding all past interest and applying a negative rate. The core scenario—a federal law addressing student loan interest for all borrowers—is preserved throughout.

original_text: The U.S. federal government introduces a public option for healthcare insurance.

variations.label: low

variations.label: medium
variations.text: The U.S. federal government introduces a public option for healthcare insurance that covers standard medical, prescription drug, and preventive care services with moderate cost-sharing.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The U.S. federal government introduces a public option for healthcare insurance that covers comprehensive medical, dental, vision, mental health, and prescription drug services with low copays and minimal deductibles.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: The U.S. federal government introduces a public option for healthcare insurance that covers all medical, dental, vision, mental health, long-term care, and prescription drug services with no premiums, no deductibles, and no out-of-pocket costs.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the breadth of services covered and decreases cost-sharing burdens, progressing monotonically from catastrophic-only coverage with high out-of-pocket costs to fully comprehensive coverage with zero cost-sharing. The core scenario—the federal government introducing a public option—remains constant across all tiers.

original_text: South Dakota bans the teaching of Critical Race Theory in public schools.

variations.label: low
variations.text: South Dakota restricts the teaching of certain Critical Race Theory concepts in K-12 public school classrooms.

variations.label: medium
variations.text: South Dakota bans the teaching of Critical Race Theory in public schools.

variations.label: high
variations.text: South Dakota bans the teaching of Critical Race Theory in all public schools and state universities, with funding penalties for noncompliance.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: South Dakota bans the teaching, promotion, or discussion of Critical Race Theory in all educational institutions, government agencies, and state-funded programs, with severe penalties for violations.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases in both scope (from voluntary guidance to all state-funded entities) and strictness (from non-binding discouragement to outright ban with severe penalties). The core scenario—South Dakota addressing CRT in education—is preserved throughout. Tier 3 closely matches the original statement.

original_text: The U.S. federal government increases funding for renewable energy research by 50%.

variations.label: low
variations.text: The U.S. federal government increases funding for renewable energy research by 50%, with the additional funds supporting mostly incremental studies that produce modest and narrowly applicable findings.

variations.label: medium
variations.text: The U.S. federal government increases funding for renewable energy research by 50%, supporting a solid portfolio of peer-reviewed studies that yield meaningful advances in the field.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The U.S. federal government increases funding for renewable energy research by 50%, enabling high-caliber, rigorously designed research programs that produce significant technological breakthroughs and widely cited publications.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: The U.S. federal government increases funding for renewable energy research by 50%, catalyzing world-class, paradigm-shifting research that delivers transformative energy technologies and reshapes the global scientific landscape.

quality_checks.reasoning: All five tiers preserve the core scenario of a 50% federal funding increase for renewable energy research. The property varied is the quality and impact of the resulting research, progressing from negligible/redundant studies (tier 1) through incremental findings (tier 2), solid meaningful advances (tier 3), significant breakthroughs (tier 4), to paradigm-shifting transformative impact (tier 5). No new structural elements are introduced; only the descriptors of research quality and impact change monotonically.

variations.label: medium
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to addiction treatment programs.

variations.label: maximal

original_text: Oregon adopts a ranked-choice voting system for state and federal elections.

variations.label: low
variations.text: Oregon adopts a ranked-choice voting system for state and federal elections with minimal voter outreach, limited poll-worker training, and only basic tabulation software in place.

variations.label: medium
variations.text: Oregon adopts a ranked-choice voting system for state and federal elections.

variations.label: high
variations.text: Oregon adopts a ranked-choice voting system for state and federal elections with a well-funded voter education campaign, certified tabulation technology, thorough poll-worker training, and a phased rollout to ensure smooth implementation.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: Oregon adopts a ranked-choice voting system for all state, federal, and local elections with a comprehensive multi-year rollout, extensive multilingual voter education, rigorously audited open-source tabulation software, independent nonpartisan oversight, robust dispute-resolution procedures, and ongoing evaluation to refine the system over time.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier describes the same core scenario—Oregon adopting RCV—while steadily increasing the quality and comprehensiveness of implementation. Tier 1 is a rushed, bare-bones adoption; tier 3 matches the original neutral statement; tier 5 represents a gold-standard, fully resourced implementation covering all election levels with robust supporting infrastructure.

original_text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free public college tuition for all students.

variations.label: medium

variations.label: high

variations.label: maximal

Tier 4 broadens what is covered beyond tuition to include fees and textbooks. Tier 5 represents maximal coverage by guaranteeing the entire cost of attendance plus a living stipend. The core scenario—federal government covering public higher education costs—is preserved throughout, with the scope and depth of coverage increasing monotonically.

original_text: The Assault Weapons Ban is renewed in the United States.
identified_property: comprehensiveness and strictness of the ban

variations.label: minimal
variations.text: A narrow restriction on a small number of specific assault-style firearm models is enacted in the United States, with existing owners fully grandfathered in.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A limited Assault Weapons Ban is renewed in the United States, covering select semi-automatic rifles by name but excluding most pistols and shotguns, with broad exemptions for current owners.

variations.label: medium
variations.text: The Assault Weapons Ban is renewed in the United States.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A strengthened Assault Weapons Ban is enacted in the United States, broadly prohibiting semi-automatic firearms with any military-style feature, banning high-capacity magazines, and restricting future sales and transfers of covered weapons.

variations.label: maximal
variations.text: A sweeping Assault Weapons Ban is enacted in the United States, prohibiting the manufacture, sale, transfer, and possession of all semi-automatic firearms and detachable magazines, with a mandatory buyback program and no grandfathering of existing weapons.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases in the breadth of weapons covered and the strictness of enforcement. Tier 1 is a narrow, model-specific restriction with full grandfathering. Tier 2 expands slightly but retains broad exemptions. Tier 3 matches the original AWB renewal. Tier 4 broadens the feature test and restricts transfers. Tier 5 is maximally comprehensive, covering all semi-automatics with mandatory buyback and no grandfathering.
